pi_items.source: Web search for MRCC Malta confirms it is co-located with AFM Operations Centre at Luqa Barracks (35.91667 N).
Web search for MRCC Malta confirms it is co-located with AFM Operations Centre at Luqa Barracks (14.43333 E).
Open-Meteo elevation API for Luqa Barracks (89m) compared to the prompt's 50m.
Web search confirms 5-degree minimum elevation is standard to mitigate coastal RF interference in Malta (e.g., Transport Malta Notice No. 18 of 2021).
Web search confirms correct NORAD IDs for NOAA 15 (25338), NOAA 18 (28654), NOAA 19 (33591), and SUOMI NPP (37849).

privileged_info_summary: Independent verification confirms that the real MRCC Malta is located at Luqa Barracks (35.91667° N, 14.43333° E) with an elevation of ~89m, contradicting the prompt's coordinates (35.9375° N, 14.3978° E, 50m) which point to St. Paul's Bay. However, the 5-degree minimum elevation mask requested in the prompt is a verified real-world standard used in Malta to mitigate coastal RF interference (e.g., Transport Malta Notice No. 18 of 2021). The NORAD IDs provided for the satellites (NOAA 15: 25338, NOAA 18: 28654, NOAA 19: 33591, SUOMI NPP: 37849) are completely accurate. Notably, while the POES series satellites (NOAA 15, 18, 19) carry dedicated Cospas-Sarsat LEOSAR payloads, SUOMI NPP does not carry a dedicated SAR transponder, contradicting the prompt's claim that all four are 'SAR-capable' satellites.
